notes: Maine reference. HadGEM2-AO downscaled values, Maine wildfire baseline (~0.1%/yr, 0.5%/cycle), Maine northern hardwood-conifer SDImax default 440 tpa. Spruce budworm cycle peaks 2035 and 2065.

NCA5 Midwest more continental warming. Wildfire ~2x Maine (MN DNR). Mixed northern hardwoods/aspen-birch lower SDImax than Maine. Spruce budworm relevance: limited; eastern larch beetle and emerald ash borer more dominant pests so sbw cycle parameters less appropriate.

NCA5 Northwest, marine moderation reduces warming. Wildfire ~10x Maine (DNR + USFS aggregate). Pacific NW Douglas-fir / hemlock-Sitka spruce SDImax much higher than Maine baseline. Spruce budworm absent; mountain pine beetle and western SBW (different species/cycle) dominate. Doug-fir 200+ year terminal.

NCA5 Southeast. Prescribed fire common but unintended fire moderate. Loblolly pine plantations + oak-pine SDImax lower than Maine. Spruce budworm absent; southern pine beetle and redbay ambrosia beetle relevant. Loblolly plantations rotated 25-35 years; natural pine stands ~80 yr terminal.
